description: Mapping of PDPA 2010 (Act 709) sections to guidelines issued by the Commissioner / Jabatan Perlindungan Data Peribadi (JPDP)

guidelines.title: Guideline on Cross-Border Personal Data Transfer 2025

guidelines.notes: The 2024 Amendment inserted the definition of 'data protection officer' into s2. The DPO Guideline elaborates on this definition, specifying the qualifications, role scope, and attributes that constitute a DPO for the purposes of the Act.

The Notice and Choice Principle requires data controllers to provide data subjects with written notice of prescribed information. The DPN Guideline sets out the format, content, language, timing, and method of delivery of data protection notices.
The Disclosure Principle requires that personal data is only disclosed for the purposes stated in the notice. The Guideline clarifies how disclosure purposes should be described in the notice.

The security principle includes the obligation to implement measures to prevent unauthorized access, loss, or destruction of data. The DBN Guideline addresses the security breach response aspect of this obligation.

Section 129A establishes the legal framework for cross-border transfers. The CBPDT Guideline 2025 provides comprehensive practical guidance on all available transfer mechanisms, including adequacy decisions, binding corporate rules, standard contractual clauses, certification, and derogations.

Section 129B (inserted by A1727) establishes the mandatory DPO requirement. The DPO Guideline provides detailed guidance on all aspects of DPO appointment, qualification, registration, and responsibilities.

Section 143A (inserted by A1727) establishes the mandatory data breach notification obligation. The DBN Guideline provides comprehensive guidance on all aspects of breach identification, assessment, notification, and record-keeping.

The data subject rights include the right of access (s30), right to correct (s34), and right to opt out from direct marketing (s43). The DPN Guideline addresses how these rights should be disclosed in data protection notices.
